CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Curves_on_s.name: CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Curves_on_surface_topology
CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Curves_on_s.brief: The class Curves_on_surface_topology provides methods to compute shortest non contractible cycles and to test homotopy on paths.
CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Curves_on_s.detailed: Each object of this class is constructed from an external mesh, either a 2D combinatorial map or a model of a FaceGraph. It maintains a correspondence between this mesh and an internal representation, computed the first time a homotopy test is called. The user must not modify the input surface as long as homotopy tests are performed with this Curves_on_surface_topology . Mesh a model of CombinatorialMap or of FaceGraph

members.name: halfedge_descriptor
members.brief: A descriptor to Dart for combinatorial/generalized maps, or a halfedge descriptor for models of the FaceGraph concept.

members.name: Curves_on_surface_topology
members.brief: creates a Curves_on_surface_topology object using amesh as input.

members.name: are_freely_homotopic
members.brief: returns true if the closed paths p1 and p2 are freely homotopic.
members.detailed: p1 and p2 must be two paths on amesh .
members.argsstring: (const Path_on_surface< Mesh > &p1, const Path_on_surface< Mesh > &p2) const

members.name: are_homotopic_with_fixed_endpoints
members.brief: returns true if the paths p1 and p2 are homotopic with fixed endpoints.
members.detailed: The paths p1 and p2 must have the same endpoints but must not be closed. Equivalent to is_contractible(q) where q is the concatenation of p1 and the reverse of p2 . p1 and p2 must be two paths on amesh .
members.argsstring: (const Path_on_surface< Mesh > &p1, const Path_on_surface< Mesh > &p2) const

members.name: is_contractible
members.brief: returns true if the closed path p is contractible.
members.detailed: p must be a closed path on amesh .

members.name: is_homotopic_to_simple_cycle
members.brief: returns true if the closed path p is homotopic to some simple cycle.
members.detailed: p must be a closed path on amesh .

members.name: compute_edge_width
members.brief: returns a non-contractible cycle of type Path_on_surface with minimal number of edges.
members.detailed: This number of edges is the edge width of the mesh.

members.name: compute_shortest_non_contractible_cycle
members.brief: returns a non-contractible cycle of type Path_on_surface with minimal length, where the length of a cycle is the sum of the weights of its edges computed thanks to the WeightFunctor wf .
members.detailed: By default, all the edge weights are set to 1 (thanks to the Unit_weight_functor functor).

members.name: compute_shortest_non_contractible_cycle_with_base_point
members.brief: returns a non-contractible cycle of type Path_on_surface with minimal length going through the source vertex of d , where the length of a cycle is the sum of the weights of its edges computed thanks to the WeightFunctor wf .
members.detailed: By default, all the edge weights are set to 1 (thanks to the Unit_weight_functor functor).

members.name: compute_face_width
members.brief: returns a vector of darts representing a non-contractible curve with a minimal number of intersection with the graph of the mesh.
members.detailed: This curve can be described by the alternating sequence of faces and vertices it goes through, so that each dart in the returned vector belongs to both a face and the next vertex in the alternating sequence. (Here, faces and vertices are viewed as subsets of darts.) The size of the returned vector is the face width of the mesh.

members.name: set_verbose
members.brief: set whether the function should output error message to std::cerr when the prerequisite of the argument(s) is not met.
members.detailed: Affects are_freely_homotopic(p1, p2) , are_homotopic_with_fixed_endpoints(p1, p2) , is_contractible(p) , and is_homotopic_to_simple_cycle(p)

CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Euclidean_l.name: CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Euclidean_length_weight_functor
CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Euclidean_l.brief: A model of WeightFunctor assigning its Euclidean length to every edge.
CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Euclidean_l.detailed: amesh should have points associated with their vertices.

members.name: Weight_t
members.brief: Number type of the weights.

members.name: Euclidean_length_weight_functor
members.brief: creates a Euclidean_length_weight_functor given a mesh.

CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Path_on_sur.name: CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Path_on_surface
CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Path_on_sur.brief: The class Path_on_surface represents a walk in a mesh which is either a model of CombinatorialMap , a model of GeneralizedMap or a model of a FaceGraph .
CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Path_on_sur.detailed: Each object of this class is constructed from an external mesh on which the path should lie. A path is represented as a sequence of darts or halfedges, each one representing an oriented edge in the path. The class Path_on_surface behaves as a container for this sequence of darts/halfedges. Elements are added in the path one at a time to the path thanks to the push_back() method. Mesh a model of CombinatorialMap , GeneralizedMap or of FaceGraph

members.name: halfedge_descriptor
members.brief: A handle to Dart for combinatorial/generalized maps, or a halfedge descriptor for models of the FaceGraph concept.

members.name: Path_on_surface
members.brief: creates an empty path object which lies on amesh .

members.name: is_empty
members.brief: returns true iff this path is empty

members.name: is_closed
members.brief: returns true iff this path is closed.

members.name: is_simple
members.brief: returns true iff this path does not pass twice through a same edge or a same vertex.

members.name: length
members.brief: returns the length of the path, i.e. its number of edges.

members.name: operator[]
members.brief: returns the ith dart of the path.
members.detailed: i< length() .

members.name: clear
members.brief: clears this path.

members.name: can_be_pushed
members.brief: returns true iff hd can be added at the end of this path. If flip is true, the direction of hd is reversed before checking

members.name: push_back
members.brief: adds hd at the end of this path.
members.detailed: If flip is true, the opposite of hd is considered. can_be_pushed(hd)

members.name: can_be_pushed_by_index
members.brief: returns true iff the dart/halfedge with index i can be added at the end of this path.
members.detailed: If Mesh is a Polyhedron_3 , takes time proportional to the number of darts/halfedges.

members.name: push_back_by_index
members.brief: adds the dart/halfedge with index i at the end of this path.
members.detailed: If Mesh is a Polyhedron_3 , takes time proportional to the number of halfedges. can_be_pushed_by_index(i)

members.name: push_back_by_index
members.brief: adds successively all dart/halfedges in l (a sequence of indices), at the end of the path.
members.detailed: If Mesh is a Polyhedron_3 , takes time proportional to the number of halfedges. For each index i , can_be_pushed_by_index(i) should be true.

members.name: extend_positive_turn
members.brief: adds the dart/halfedge obtained by turning nb times around the target vertex of the last dart/halfedge in this path, in the positive circular order.
members.detailed: To extend with a positive 1 turn thus amounts to extend with the next() pointer. (A zero turn corresponds to the opposite() pointer.) ! is_empty()

members.name: extend_negative_turn
members.brief: adds the dart/halfedge obtained by turning nb times around the target vertex of the last dart/halfedge in this path, in the negative circular order.
members.detailed: To extend with a negative 1 turn thus amounts to extend with the composite pointer: opposite(prev(opposite())) . ! is_empty()

members.name: can_be_pushed_by_label
members.brief: returns true iff the dart/halfedge with label l can be added at the end of this path.
members.detailed: Mesh must be a model of PolygonalSchema concept.

members.name: push_back_by_label
members.brief: adds successively all darts/halfedges in s (a sequence of labels separated by spaces) at the end of this path.
members.detailed: For each label, l, can_be_pushed_by_label(l) should be true. Mesh must be a model of PolygonalSchema concept.

members.name: operator+=
members.brief: concatenates other to this path.
members.detailed: the last vertex of this path should coincide with the first vertex of other .

members.name: reverse
members.brief: reverses this path (i.e. negates its orientation).

members.name: generate_random_path
members.brief: creates a random open path with length darts/halfedges.

members.name: generate_random_closed_path
members.brief: creates a random closed path with at least length darts/halfedges.

CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_s.name: CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_schema_min_items
CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_s.brief: The class Polygonal_schema_min_items defines a struct with a std::string as the information associated with darts, and no attribute is enabled.
CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_s.detailed: Is model of PolygonalSchemaItems Example The following example shows one implementation of the Polygonal_schema_min_items class. struct Polygonal_schema_min_items { template < class PS> struct Dart_wrapper { struct Info_for_darts { std::string m_label; }; typedef Info_for_darts Dart_info; }; }; CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_schema_with_combinatorial_map <Items,Alloc> CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_schema_with_generalized_map <Items,Alloc>

CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_s.name: CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_schema_with_combinatorial_map
CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_s.brief: The class Polygonal_schema_with_combinatorial_map is a model of PolygonalSchema using Combinatorial_map as underlying model.
CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_s.detailed: Items a model of the PolygonalSchemaItems concept. Equal to Polygonal_schema_min_items by default. Alloc has to match the standard allocator requirements. The rebind mechanism Alloc will be used to create appropriate allocators internally with value type Dart . Equal to CGAL_ALLOCATOR(int) from <CGAL/memory.h> by default.

CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_s.name: CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_schema_with_generalized_map
CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_s.brief: The class Polygonal_schema_with_generalized_map is a model of PolygonalSchema using Generalized_map as underlying model.
CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_s.detailed: Items a model of the PolygonalSchemaItems concept. Equal to Polygonal_schema_min_items by default. Alloc has to match the standard allocator requirements. The rebind mechanism Alloc will be used to create appropriate allocators internally with value type Dart . Equal to CGAL_ALLOCATOR(int) from <CGAL/memory.h> by default.

CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Unit_weight.name: CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Unit_weight_functor
CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Unit_weight.brief: A model of WeightFunctor assigning unit weight to every edge.

members.name: Weight_t
members.brief: Number type of the weights.

PolygonalSchema.name: PolygonalSchema
PolygonalSchema.brief: The concept PolygonalSchema defines a 2D polygonal schema, i.e. a combinatorial surface with labeled edges. A PolygonalSchema is created incrementally by adding facets one at a time. A label is any word, that does not contain a space.
PolygonalSchema.detailed: PolygonalSchema::Dart_info should be a class having a public data member std::string m_label. PolygonalSchema::dimension should be equal to 2. Refines GenericMap Has models CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_schema_with_combinatorial_map<Items,Alloc> CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_schema_with_generalized_map<Items,Alloc>

members.name: PolygonalSchema
members.brief: creates an empty PolygonalSchema object.

members.name: init_facet
members.brief: starts a new facet.

members.name: finish_facet
members.brief: finishes the current facet.
members.detailed: Returns the first dart of this facet. A facet is under creation.

members.name: add_edge_to_facet
members.brief: adds one edge to the current facet, given by its label l (any string containing no space, using minus sign for orientation).
members.detailed: Since the surface is oriented, each label can be used only twice with opposite signs. If this method is called with a label already used, with same sign, an error message is given and this label is ignored. A facet is under creation.

members.name: add_edges_to_facet
members.brief: adds the given edges to the current facet.
members.detailed: s is a sequence of labels, separated by spaces. All the corresponding edges are added into the current facet. A facet is under creation.

members.name: add_facet
members.brief: adds directly one facet giving the sequence of labels s of all its edges (labels are separated by spaces).

members.name: get_label
members.brief: returns the label of dart d .

members.name: get_dart_labeled
members.brief: returns dart with label s , NULL if this label is not used.

members.name: is_perforated
members.brief: returns true iff the facet containing d is perforated.

members.name: is_perforated
members.brief: Shortcut for is_perforated(get_dart_labeled(s)) .

members.name: perforate_facet
members.brief: perforates the facet containing d . Returns the number of darts of the face; 0 if the facet was already perforated.

members.name: perforate_facet
members.brief: Shortcut for perforate_facet(get_dart_labeled(s)).

members.name: fill_facet
members.brief: fills the facet containing d . Returns the number of darts of the face; 0 if the facet was already filled.

members.name: fill_facet
members.brief: Shortcut for fill_facet(get_dart_labeled(s)) .

PolygonalSchemaItems.name: PolygonalSchemaItems
PolygonalSchemaItems.brief: The concept PolygonalSchemaItems allows to customize a PolygonalSchema by choosing the information associated with darts, and by enabling and disabling some attributes. Dart_wrapper<Map>::Dart_info , should be a class having a public data member std::string m_label.

PkgSurfaceMeshTopologyRef.name: PkgSurfaceMeshTopologyRef
PkgSurfaceMeshTopologyRef.detailed: Guillaume Damiand and Francis Lazarus This package provides a toolbox for manipulating curves on a combinatorial surface from the topological point of view. Two main functionalities are proposed. One is the computation of shortest curves that cannot be continuously deformed to a point. This includes the computation of the so-called edge width and face width of the vertex-edge graph of a combinatorial surface. The other functionality is the homotopy test for deciding if two given curves on a combinatorial surface can be continuously deformed one into the other. Introduced in: CGAL 5.1 BibTeX: cgal:dl-smtopology-26a License: GPL Classified Reference Pages Concepts PolygonalSchema PolygonalSchemaItems WeightFunctor Classes CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Curves_on_surface_topology <Mesh> CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Path_on_surface <Mesh> CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_schema_with_combinatorial_map <Items,Alloc> CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_schema_with_generalized_map <Items,Alloc> CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Polygonal_schema_min_items CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Unit_weight_functor CGAL::Surface_mesh_topology::Euclidean_length_weight_functor <Mesh> Draw a Mesh with Paths CGAL::draw<Mesh>()

members.name: draw
members.brief: opens a new window and draws amesh , either a 2D linear cell complex or a model of the FaceGraph concept, plus the paths lying on this mesh given in apaths .
members.detailed: Parameters of the drawing are taken from the optional graphics scene options parameter. A call to this function blocks the execution of the program until the drawing window is closed. This function requires CGAL_Qt6 , and is only available if the macro CGAL_USE_BASIC_VIEWER is defined. Linking with the cmake target CGAL::CGAL_Basic_viewer will link with CGAL_Qt6 and add the definition CGAL_USE_BASIC_VIEWER . Mesh either a 2D linear cell complex or a model of the FaceGraph concept. GSOptions a model of GraphicsSceneOptionsFaceGraphWithPaths concept. amesh the mesh to draw. apaths the paths to draw, which should lie on amesh . gso the graphics scene options parameter.
members.argsstring: (const Mesh &amesh, const std::vector< Surface_mesh_topology::Path_on_surface< Mesh > > &apaths, const GSOptions &gso)

members.name: draw
members.brief: A shortcut to CGAL::draw (amesh, apaths, Graphics_scene_options_face_graph_with_paths{}) .

members.name: draw
members.brief: Same function than draw() but taking the paths from a list instead from an std::vector.
members.argsstring: (const Mesh &amesh, std::initializer_list< Path_on_surface< Mesh > > apaths, const GSOptions &gso)

members.name: draw
members.brief: A shortcut to CGAL::draw (amesh, apaths, Graphics_scene_options_face_graph_with_paths{}) .

members.name: add_to_graphics_scene
members.brief: adds the vertices, edges and faces of amesh , either a 2D linear cell complex or a model of the FaceGraph concept, plus the paths lying on this mesh given in apaths , into the given graphic scene gs .
members.detailed: Parameters of the cells are taken from the optional graphics scene options parameter gso . Note that gs is not cleared before being filled (to enable to draw several data structures in the same basic viewer). Mesh either a 2D linear cell complex or a model of the FaceGraph concept. GSOptions a model of GraphicsSceneOptionsFaceGraphWithPaths concept. amesh the mesh to draw. gs the graphic scene to fill. gso the graphics scene options parameter. apaths the paths to draw, which should lie on amesh .
members.argsstring: (const Mesh &amesh, CGAL::Graphics_scene &gs, const GSOptions &gso, const std::vector< Surface_mesh_topology::Path_on_surface< Mesh > > &apaths)

members.name: add_to_graphics_scene
members.brief: A shortcut to CGAL::add_to_graphics_scene (amesh, gs, Graphics_scene_options_face_graph_with_paths{}, apaths) .
members.argsstring: (const Mesh &amesh, CGAL::Graphics_scene &gs, const std::vector< Surface_mesh_topology::Path_on_surface< Mesh > > &apaths)

members.name: add_to_graphics_scene
members.brief: Same function than add_to_graphics_scene() but taking the paths from a list instead from an std::vector.
members.argsstring: (const Mesh &amesh, CGAL::Graphics_scene &gs, const GSOptions &gso, std::initializer_list< Path_on_surface< Mesh > > apaths)

members.name: add_to_graphics_scene
members.brief: A shortcut to CGAL::add_to_graphics_scene (amesh, gs, Graphics_scene_options_face_graph_with_paths{}, apaths) .
members.argsstring: (const Mesh &amesh, CGAL::Graphics_scene &gs, std::initializer_list< Path_on_surface< Mesh > > apaths)
